findings.title: Reset tokens are generated with predictable, non-cryptographic randomness
Reset-token verification does not consume the token, so it is reusable until expiry
findings.quote: if (!record || record.used || record.expiresAt < Date.now()) return false;

reasoning: bcrypt cost 12 is used for password hashing and bcrypt.compare for verification. Reset-token comparison uses timingSafeEqual, and the password reset limiter is mounted before its route. However, reset tokens use Date.now and Math.random instead of a CSPRNG, and verification checks record.used without ever marking the record used.
